description_long: This object is a classic Soviet RG-5 hand grenade, featuring a spherical olive-green metal body with a prominent black seam running horizontally around its midsection. The upper section is fitted with a cylindrical black safety lever and a metal pull ring, which are standard components for arming and deploying the grenade. Cyrillic text is stenciled in white on the side, indicating its model designation. The surface of the grenade is matte and slightly textured, with minor scuffs and wear visible, suggesting it is designed for rugged use. The overall construction appears robust, with the safety lever and pin assembly securely attached to the top. This type of grenade is typically used in military or paramilitary urban scenarios and is designed to be thrown by hand.
description: A spherical olive-green RG-5 hand grenade with a black safety lever, pull ring, and Cyrillic markings, featuring a matte, slightly textured metal surface.
description_view_0: Front view showing the safety lever, pull ring, and Cyrillic text on the olive-green body.
description_view_1: Rear view with the safety lever and pull ring visible, but no text or markings.
description_view_2: Side view highlighting the Cyrillic text and the full curvature of the grenade's body.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the safety lever and ring, with no visible text.
attribute_detonation_mechanism: Detonation is initiated by pulling the metal ring to release the safety lever, which triggers the internal fuse mechanism.
